title: Username field populated with Arabic RTL text renders and submits correctly in a LTR-oriented form layout
description: RTL Unicode text (Arabic script) entered into a LTR-layout username field can expose multiple bug classes: visual rendering corruption (text direction overrides adjacent fields), incorrect string comparison if the server normalizes direction markers, or backend rejection of non-ASCII usernames where they should be allowed. Systems using usernames (not just email) must handle Unicode usernames per RFC 8265.
expected_result: PASS: The Arabic username is transmitted to the server unmodified, the server performs a byte-accurate match, the user is authenticated, and the surrounding form layout remains intact during text entry. FAIL: (a) The form shows a validation error rejecting non-ASCII characters in the username field; (b) RTL text causes the password field or submit button to visually shift to the wrong side of the form; (c) the server normalizes or truncates the Arabic string causing an authentication failure for a valid account.

test_steps.expected: Login page is displayed with username and password inputs

test_steps.action: Click the username field to focus it
test_steps.expected: Username field receives focus; cursor is visible

test_steps.action: Type the Arabic RTL username into the username field
test_steps.expected: The Arabic string 'أحمد_المستخدم' is entered verbatim; the field may visually right-align text but the surrounding form layout (labels, password field, submit button) must remain correctly positioned in LTR order

test_steps.action: Verify the password field label and position are not displaced by the RTL content in the username field
test_steps.expected: Password field and its label are still visible and correctly positioned — RTL text in one field must not bleed its directionality into adjacent DOM elements
Password field accepts input without visual distortion
Form submits the Arabic username as-is without stripping, reversing, or re-encoding the Unicode characters

test_steps.action: Verify successful authentication with the Arabic username account
test_steps.expected: User is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard; the displayed username or greeting reflects the correct Arabic string
